Muppavarapu Venkaiah Naidu  (1949-07-01) 1 July 1949 (age 76)  [Chavatapalem](/wiki/Nellore_district "Nellore district"), [Madras Province](/wiki/Madras_State "Madras State"), [Dominion of India](/wiki/Dominion_of_India "Dominion of India") (present-day [Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh"), India)

(born 1 July 1949) is an Indian politician who served as the [vice president of India](/wiki/Vice_president_of_India "Vice president of India") from 2017 to 2022.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) He has also served as the [minister of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation](/wiki/Ministry_of_Housing_and_Urban_Poverty_Alleviation "Ministry of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation"), [Urban Development](/wiki/Ministry_of_Urban_Development_(India) "Ministry of Urban Development (India)") and [Information and Broadcasting](/wiki/Ministry_of_Information_and_Broadcasting_(India) "Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (India)") in the [Modi Cabinet](/wiki/First_Modi_ministry "First Modi ministry").[[6]](#cite_note-ref-6)

**Muppavarapu Venkaiah Naidu** (born 1 July 1949) is an Indian politician who served as the [vice president of India](/wiki/Vice_president_of_India "Vice president of India") from 2017 to 2022.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) He has also served as the [minister of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation](/wiki/Ministry_of_Housing_and_Urban_Poverty_Alleviation "Ministry of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation"), [Urban Development](/wiki/Ministry_of_Urban_Development_(India) "Ministry of Urban Development (India)") and [Information and Broadcasting](/wiki/Ministry_of_Information_and_Broadcasting_(India) "Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (India)") in the [Modi Cabinet](/wiki/First_Modi_ministry "First Modi ministry").[[6]](#cite_note-ref-6)

Naidu has also served as the national president of [Bharatiya Janata Party](/wiki/Bharatiya_Janata_Party "Bharatiya Janata Party") from 2002 to 2004.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Earlier, he was the Union Cabinet Minister for Rural Development in the [Atal Bihari Vajpayee government](/wiki/Third_Vajpayee_ministry "Third Vajpayee ministry").[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) He took the oath as the vice president of India and the chairman of the [Rajya Sabha](/wiki/Rajya_Sabha "Rajya Sabha") (ex-officio as the Vice President) on 11 August 2017.[[10]](#cite_note-10) In 2024, The Government of India honoured him with [Padma Vibhushan](/wiki/Padma_Vibhushan "Padma Vibhushan"), India's second highest civilian award.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Venkaiah_Naidu&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Venkaiah Naidu was born on 1 July 1949 at Chavatapalem village (near [Venkatachalam](/wiki/Venkatachalam_mandal "Venkatachalam mandal")) in [Nellore district](/wiki/Nellore_district "Nellore district") of [Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh"), to Rangaiah Naidu and Ramanamma.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14) He completed his schooling from [Zilla Parshad High School](/wiki/Zilla_Parishad_High_School "Zilla Parishad High School"), [Bucchireddy Palem](/wiki/Buchireddypalem "Buchireddypalem") (Nellore), and pursued his bachelor's degree in politics and diplomatic studies at V. R. College, [Nellore](/wiki/Nellore "Nellore"). Later, he acquired a bachelor's degree in law with specialisation in international law from [Andhra University College of Law](/wiki/Andhra_University_College_of_Law "Andhra University College of Law"), [Visakhapatnam](/wiki/Visakhapatnam "Visakhapatnam").[[15]](#cite_note-mvn12-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) He was a *swayamsevak* in the [Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh](/wiki/Rashtriya_Swayamsevak_Sangh "Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh") and joined [ABVP](/wiki/Akhil_Bharatiya_Vidyarthi_Parishad "Akhil Bharatiya Vidyarthi Parishad") during his college days. He was elected as the president of the students' union of colleges affiliated with the [Andhra University](/wiki/Andhra_University "Andhra University"). He came into the spotlight for his prominent role in the [Jai Andhra Movement](/wiki/1972_Jai_Andhra_movement "1972 Jai Andhra movement") of 1972. While [Kakani Venkata Ratnam](/wiki/Kakani_Venkata_Ratnam "Kakani Venkata Ratnam") led the movement from [Vijayawada](/wiki/Vijayawada "Vijayawada"), Naidu took active part in the agitation in Nellore, until it was called off a year later.

In 1974, he became the convener of [Jayaprakash Narayan](/wiki/Jayaprakash_Narayan "Jayaprakash Narayan") Chhatra Sangharsh Samiti of Andhra Pradesh. He took to the streets in protest against [the Emergency](/wiki/The_Emergency_(India) "The Emergency (India)") and was imprisoned. From 1977 to 1980, he was president of its youth wing.

## Political career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Venkaiah_Naidu&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Political career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c5/Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_taking_charge_as_the_Union_Minister_for_Urban_Development%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_May_28%2C_2014.jpg/250px-Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_taking_charge_as_the_Union_Minister_for_Urban_Development%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_May_28%2C_2014.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_taking_charge_as_the_Union_Minister_for_Urban_Development,_in_New_Delhi_on_May_28,_2014.jpg)

Venkaiah Naidu taking charge as the Union [Minister for Urban Development](/wiki/Minister_of_Urban_Development_(India) "Minister of Urban Development (India)") in New Delhi on 28 May 2014

Both as a student leader and political figure, Naidu gained prominence as an orator who championed the cause of the farmers and the development of backward areas. His oratory skills and political activism propelled his political career, and he was elected as an MLA to the [Andhra Pradesh Legislative Assembly](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh_Legislative_Assembly "Andhra Pradesh Legislative Assembly") twice from [Udaygiri constituency](/wiki/Udayagiri_(Assembly_constituency) "Udayagiri (Assembly constituency)") in Nellore district in [1978](/wiki/1978_Andhra_Pradesh_Legislative_Assembly_election "1978 Andhra Pradesh Legislative Assembly election") and [1983](/wiki/1983_Andhra_Pradesh_Legislative_Assembly_election "1983 Andhra Pradesh Legislative Assembly election"). He rose to become one of the most popular leaders of the BJP in Andhra Pradesh.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

After serving in various organisational posts of the BJP at the state and national level, he was elected as a member of the [Rajya Sabha](/wiki/Rajya_Sabha "Rajya Sabha") from [Karnataka](/wiki/Karnataka "Karnataka") in 1998.[[18]](#cite_note-mvn11-18) He was re-elected twice, in 2004 and 2010, from Karnataka.[[19]](#cite_note-mvn_8-19)[[20]](#cite_note-mvn10-20) He served as the party spokesperson from 1996 to 2000, bringing to the job his panache for quirky alliterations and similes. Unlike most politicians from southern India, Naidu made an effort to master Hindi, going on to address public rallies in northern India.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

After the [NDA](/wiki/National_Democratic_Alliance "National Democratic Alliance") victory in the 1999 general elections, he became the Union Cabinet Minister for Rural Development in the government headed by Prime Minister [Atal Bihari Vajpayee](/wiki/Atal_Bihari_Vajpayee "Atal Bihari Vajpayee").[[22]](#cite_note-mvn1-22) He was known for aggressively pushing for reforms in rural development and for the many schemes introduced during this period such as the '[Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana](/wiki/Pradhan_Mantri_Gram_Sadak_Yojana "Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana")'.[[23]](#cite_note-mvn2-23)[[24]](#cite_note-mvn3-24)[[25]](#cite_note-mvn4-25)

### National President of the BJP (2002–2004)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Venkaiah_Naidu&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: National President of the BJP (2002–2004)")]

Naidu succeeded [Jana Krishnamurthi](/wiki/Jana_Krishnamurthi "Jana Krishnamurthi") as the National President of the [Bharatiya Janata Party](/wiki/Bharatiya_Janata_Party "Bharatiya Janata Party") in 2003.[[26]](#cite_note-mvn5-26) On 28 January 2004, he was elected unopposed for a full three-year term.[[27]](#cite_note-mvn6-27) After the defeat of the BJP-led [NDA](/wiki/National_Democratic_Alliance "National Democratic Alliance") in the 2004 general elections, he resigned from his post on 18 October 2004 and was succeeded by [L. K. Advani](/wiki/L._K._Advani "L. K. Advani").[[28]](#cite_note-mvn_7-28) However, he remained at the forefront of the BJP as one of its senior vice-presidents and an important campaigner. Naidu raised the "Special Status to Andhra Pradesh" issue in Rajya Sabha (as opposition member in February 2014) and demanded special category state status to Andhra Pradesh. The Prime Minister agreed to it, though it was not included in the [Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh_Reorganisation_Act "Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act").

### First Modi ministry (2014–2017)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Venkaiah_Naidu&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: First Modi ministry (2014–2017)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e5/Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_taking_charge_as_the_Union_Minister_for_Parliamentary_Affairs%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_May_28%2C_2014.jpg/250px-Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_taking_charge_as_the_Union_Minister_for_Parliamentary_Affairs%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_May_28%2C_2014.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_taking_charge_as_the_Union_Minister_for_Parliamentary_Affairs,_in_New_Delhi_on_May_28,_2014.jpg)

Venkaiah Naidu taking charge as the Union Minister for Parliamentary Affairs in New Delhi on 28 May 2014

Following the victory of the BJP in the 2014 general elections, he was sworn in as the Minister for Urban Development and Parliamentary Affairs on 26 May 2014.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

Naidu is also involved with the Swarna Bharat Trust, a social service organisation founded by him in Nellore.[[30]](#cite_note-venkaiah_sbt-30) The trust runs a school for poor, orphaned and special-needs children and imparts self-employment training programsme, especially for women and youth.

He was nominated by the BJP on 29 May 2016 for the [Rajya Sabha](/wiki/Rajya_Sabha "Rajya Sabha") from Rajasthan and was elected.

## Vice President of India (2017–2022)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Venkaiah_Naidu&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Vice President of India (2017–2022)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/60/The_President%2C_Shri_Ram_Nath_Kovind_administering_the_oath_of_office_of_the_Vice_President_to_Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu%2C_at_a_Swearing-in-Ceremony%2C_at_Rashtrapati_Bhavan%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_August_11%2C_2017.jpg/250px-thumbnail.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_President,_Shri_Ram_Nath_Kovind_administering_the_oath_of_office_of_the_Vice_President_to_Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu,_at_a_Swearing-in-Ceremony,_at_Rashtrapati_Bhavan,_in_New_Delhi_on_August_11,_2017.jpg)

Swearing of Vice President-elect Venkaiah Naidu at [Rashtrapati Bhavan](/wiki/Rashtrapati_Bhavan "Rashtrapati Bhavan") Durbar Hall, 11 August 2017

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/77/The_Vice_President%2C_Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_assuming_office_at_the_Vice_President_Secretariat%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_August_11%2C_2017.jpg/250px-The_Vice_President%2C_Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_assuming_office_at_the_Vice_President_Secretariat%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_August_11%2C_2017.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Vice_President,_Shri_M._Venkaiah_Naidu_assuming_office_at_the_Vice_President_Secretariat,_in_New_Delhi_on_August_11,_2017.jpg)

The [Vice President](/wiki/Vice_President_of_India "Vice President of India"), Shri M. Venkaiah Naidu assuming office at the Vice President Secretariat, in New Delhi on 11 August 2017

On 5 July 2016, he concurrently served as Minister of Information and Broadcasting. A year later, he resigned from both offices to contest the [2017 vice presidential election](/wiki/2017_Indian_vice_presidential_election "2017 Indian vice presidential election"). He won the election to become India's 13th Vice President.[[31]](#cite_note-31) He obtained 516 votes against UPA candidate [Gopalkrishna Gandhi](/wiki/Gopalkrishna_Gandhi "Gopalkrishna Gandhi"), who received 244 votes. As his tenure ended, Naidu decided to move back to Hyderabad with his family and declared that he will resume his career in the service of the nation.[[32]](#cite_note-32) He was succeeded by [Jagdeep Dhankhar](/wiki/Jagdeep_Dhankhar "Jagdeep Dhankhar") on 11 August 2022.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Venkaiah_Naidu&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Naidu married Usha on 14 April 1972,[[33]](#cite_note-33) with whom he has a son and a daughter.[[34]](#cite_note-34)[[35]](#cite_note-35) Usha served as the 13th Second lady of India.[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-37)
